raw:
- there exists a mesh networking protocol called reticulum that is uncannily close to what I have outlined here. Reticulum will be bridge between the main package and the beta package. The main package is meant to be ultimately versatile. The Beta is meant to catch the tech wave of the next decade.

- Reticulum shouold be implemented into the main build of discord as just another source.

- concord is meant to be a swiss army knife of self-hosted comms. For this phase it connects to any and all matrix instances, any and all discord servers, and any and all concord instances.

- The reticulum connection is a beta feature that we will implement into the main build when we are ready to welcome out userbase onto the fully mesh suported platform that the beta aims for.
optimized:
### OPT-001: Reticulum as Beta Network Backend
Implement Reticulum (https://reticulum.network/) as the networking layer for a dedicated beta build of Concord. This beta targets full mesh networking as the primary transport. Reticulum is not yet part of the main build — it is a staging ground for mesh-first architecture to be promoted when the userbase is ready.

Source: Instructions 1 + 4

---

### OPT-002: Concord Main Build — Universal Comms Aggregator
Concord's main build must function as a swiss-army-knife self-hosted communications hub. For the current phase, it must connect to:
- Any Matrix homeserver (federated Matrix protocol support)
- Any Discord server (Discord as a source/bridge)
- Any other Concord instance (Concord-to-Concord peering)

No Reticulum in this build yet.

Source: Instruction 3

---

### OPT-003: Discord Integration via Source Model
Add Discord as a first-class source in Concord's main build using the same source/bridge model used for Matrix. Discord is not a special case — it is just another source. Implement it accordingly.

Source: Instruction 2

---

### OPT-004: Reticulum Promotion Path (Architecture Constraint)
Design the Reticulum integration (OPT-001) such that it can be merged into the main build cleanly when the beta matures. The beta is the proving ground; the main build is the eventual target. Do not design them as permanently divergent codebases.

Source: Instructions 1 + 4
